stem: Match each J277 algorithm to the description that best identifies it.
review_notes: One-to-one matching. Each algorithm has a distinguishing description; ordering is jumbled in the right column to discourage rote alignment.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Match each algorithm to its description.

right: Checks each item from the start in turn until the target is found
Halves the remaining search space each step; only works on sorted data
Compares each pair of adjacent items and swaps if out of order, repeating in passes
Builds a sorted section one item at a time by shifting larger items to make room
Splits the list in half recursively until each part has one item, then merges them in order

mark_points.text: Linear search — checks each item from the start
Binary search — halves a sorted search space
Insertion sort — builds a sorted section by shifting
Merge sort — split recursively then merge
